IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT AHMMED HAS RECEIVED RESPONSES TO HIS DOCUMENT DEMAND. ANY DELAY WAS OCCASIONED BY AMBIGUITY IN AHMMED'S PROOF OF SERVICE. NO SANCTIONS. HAD FAILURE TO TIMELY RESPOND BEEN PROVED, THE COURT WOULD HAVE ENTERTAINED A MOTION UNDER CCP 2031.300(A) IN ANY EVENT. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Compel Objection Free Response From Pltf And X-Deft One Rincon Hill Association To X-Complainant'S Demand For Inspection And Production Of Docs, Set One, And, Monetary Sanctions Against Association And Its Atty Of Record; Memo Of P&A In Support Of
Off Calendar Oct-28-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Aaron Hayes, Esq. (415-689-1227) of Freeman Mathis & Gary for Plaintiff/ opposing party One Rincon Hill Association.
No other Appearances.

Plaintiff does not contest the tentative ruling.The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MOHSIN AHMMED's Motion To Compel Objection Free Response From Plaintiff And Cross-Defendant One Rincon Hill Association To Cross-Complainant'S Demand For Inspection And Production Of Documents, Set One, And, Monetary Sanctions Against Association And Its Atty Of Record is DENIED. It is undisputed that Ahmmed has received responses to his document demand. Any delay was occasioned by ambiguity in Ahmmed's proof of service. No sanctions. Had failure to timely respond been proved, the Court would have entertained a motion under CCP 2031.300(a) in any event. Order is signed in Open Court. (see scanned order for details)Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-23-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John Fitzgerald, Esq.(415-689-1209) for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant/Moving party One Rincon Hill.
Shawn Parr, Esq. (408-267-4500) for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant/Moving party Pro Solutions.
Mohsin Ahhmmed (415-314-1715) for Defendant/Cross-Complainant/opposing party in Pro Per.
Court reporter: Jaimie Hopp, (415-433-5777) CSR #13751.

Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant argues the 9th Cause of action of Conspiracy. Defendant ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT PRO SOLUTIONS, ONE RINCON HILL ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) Though not always artfully drafted, the pro per cross-complaint is sufficient notice pleading. A general demurrer (1) does not lie to only part of a cause of action, (2) is not sustained when an improper remedy is sought and (3) is overcome by any valid cause of action regardless of what it may be labeled. Special demurrers are sustained only when a complaint is so bad that the responding party cannot determine what issues must be admitted or denied. Cross Defendant has 40 days from today to file answer and waives notice of ruling. Order is signed in open court. (see scanned order for details) Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-10-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT MOHSIN AHMMED'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Defendant's Request for Judicial notice is DENIED. Defendant raises issues of fact that cannot be properly addressed on demurrer. The Court notes that Plaintiff's opposition is filed and served late and not in conformity with CCP 1005. However, opposition is considered as Defendant addressed it on the merits in the reply. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.
